properties.remark: PRAIRIE COUNTY DES REPORTS 8 MILES SOUTH OF TERRY THUNDERSTORM WIND DAMAGE TO A SHED AND HORSE TRAILER.
HAIL FALLING FOR 10 MINS...STILL FALLING AT TIME OF CALL
DIME TO QUARTER SIZED HAIL...STARTED HAILING ABOUT 5 MINS BEFORE THE CALL. HAIL IS ACCUMULATING
DIME TO QUARTER SIZED HAIL...STARTED HAILING ABOUT 5 MINS BEFORE THE CALL. HAIL IS ACCUMULATING
HAIL FALLING FOR 10 MINS...STILL FALLING AT TIME OF CALL
QUARTER SIZED HAIL. HEAVY RAIN.
MEASURED 65 MPH WIND SPEED GUST AT 312 AM. 0.46 INCHES OF PRECIP.
THE DECK IS COVERED IN PEA SIZED HAIL. THE YARD HAS 5 TO 8 INCHES OF WATER COVERING IT. THE STREETS AND SIDEWALKS ARE FLOODING.
HIGHWAY 2 HAS 4 TO 5 INCHES OF WATER RUNNING OVER IT. MOST ROADS IN TOWN ARE FLOODED WITH SOME UP TO 1 FOOT DEEP. THE RAILROAD UNDERPASS IS FLOODED AND HAS BEEN CLOSED OFF BY LAW ENFORCEMENT.
KAMPFER DRIVE IS FLOODED. KAMPFER CREEK IS SUBSTANTIALLY FLOODING. THERE IS STANDING WATER NEAR THE FISH AND GAME BUILDING.
WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 50 MPH AFTER MIDNIGHT. DIME SIZED HAIL FELL FOR ABOUT 3 MINUTES. A TALL HEALTHY COTTONWOOD TREE WAS UPROOTED OVER NIGHT.
WATER FLOWING 3 TO 5 FEET DEEP DOWN TWO COULEES ON PROPERTY AND THEN SEVERAL INCHES DEEP ACROSS RIVERSIDE DRIVE.
BILLINSLEY ROAD NEAR ANTELOPE CREEK IS CLOSED. WATER IS RUNNING OVER THE ROAD.
WATER IS OVER THE ROAD ON ROOSEVELT TRAIL BETWEEN HIGHWAY 24 AND LINDELL ROAD.
WATER FROM CHERRY CREEK IS NOW FLOODING BASEMENTS. WATER IS 3 TO 4 INCHES UP ONTO THE FOUNDATION.

CHERRY CREEK IS BEGINNING TO FLOOD SOME HOMES ALONG SKYLARK ROAD IN GLASGOW. THE SUNNYSIDE GOLF COURSE IS UNDER WATER. FLOOD WATERS HAVE REACHED THE EDGE OF US HIGHWAY 2 ON THE WEST SIDE OF GLASGOW. MARTIN COULEE ROAD IS FLOODED.
6 TO 7 INCHES OF WATER OVER ROADWAY IN WHITEWATER. WATER IS RECEDING AT 630 PM.
GEER RD...APX 11 MILES NORTH OF NASHUA...WASHED OUT AND CLOSED.
SARGENT CREEK...11 MILES NORTH OF NASHUA IS RUNNING OUT OF ITS BANKS
SARGENT CREEK...11 MILES NORTH OF NASHUA IS RUNNING OUT OF ITS BANKS
EAST FORK OF THE WOLF CREEK IS UP AND FLOWING OUT OF ITS BANKS. STILL RAINING VERY HARD.
MANY STREETS IN WOLF POINT ARE FLOODING. THE UNDERPASS TO DOWNTOWN IS QUICKLY FILLING WITH WATER.
A COUPLE OF CULVERTS WERE WASHED OUT DUE TO AN ESTIMATED 6 INCHES OF HEAVY RAIN OCCURRING ABOUT 4 MILES NORTHWEST TO NORTH OF THIS LOCATION.
SEVERAL PEA SIZED STONES WITH A FEW LARGER. THE LARGEST STONE WAS 1 INCH IN DIAMETER.

FRENCH LANE -2 MILES NORTH OF SCOBEY- UNDER WATER IN ABOUT 5 PLACES. HWY 248 NEAR FOUR BUTTES HAD WATER OVER THE ROAD. MIDDLE FORK OF POPLAR NEAR SCOBEY FLOODING. AS OF ABOUT 230 PM HAD ABOUT 2.00 INCHES OF PRECIP.
NUMEROUS CREEKS ARE FLOODING OR RUNNING FULL
SEVERAL ROADS FLOODING AND COULEES FULL

WHEN PERSON ARRIVED AT HOME THE CREEK AROUND HIS HOUSE WAS OUT OF IT BANKS AND WATER WAS RUNNING DOWN THE HILLS AND THROUGH HIS YARD. AN HOUR LATER WATER WAS STILL RUNNING THROUGH THE YARD. HAD 1.10 INCHES OF RAINFALL FROM THIS STORM.
0.50 INCHES OF RAIN
WINDS SUSTAINED 50-60 MPH.
ESTIMATED BASEBALL SIZE HAIL KNOCKING HORSES OVER.
ESTIMATED GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL.

65 MPH GUST RECORDED AT THIS LOCATION. WINDS GOT STRONGER DESTROYING THE WIND GAGE. THE WINDS KNOCKED DOWN TREES AND DAMAGED TWO 50 YEAR OLD GARAGES. THE WINDS BLEW AWAY A GRAINERY AS WELL AS A NATURAL GAS METER. WIND DAMAGE TO CROPS ALSO REPORTED AT THIS LOCATION AS WELL AS QUARTER SIZE HAIL. THE HIGH WIND EVENT LASTED 35-40 MINUTES.
